summary: Review of the c0eda157 commit addressing sourcery-ai[bot] external review #1. Two external bugs were fixed (literal-scan self-trip in scripts/grep-keystone-api-residue.sh; missing verb in docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md) and one high-level suggestion was applied (rename apiResourceName → subResourceName across the helper, all 5 call sites, tests, doc comments, and the chaos-e2e fixture). The script was also hardened with an EXCLUDED_ROOTS array plus a top-level-directory completeness guard that exits 2 on a newly added unclassified directory. The rename is mechanical and complete: only one residual textual reference to apiResourceName remains, intentionally, in the helper's doc-comment to document the rename. Residue scanner reports clean (30 occurrences, all annotated). go vet and the renamed unit tests pass.

tests_checklist.item: Test names updated atomically with the helper rename (no stale Test*ApiResourceName* identifier)
Edge cases covered (empty CR name, CR name containing -api literally)
Residue scanner self-test: bash scripts/grep-keystone-api-residue.sh exits 0 on the current tree

code_quality_checklist.item: Helper rename propagated to every call site, test, doc comment, and downstream fixture
No partial rename: grep for apiResourceName returns only the single intentional doc-comment reference at reconcile_hpa.go:29
Search-root list now has explicit exclusion rationales (architecture, internal, scripts, releases, images, LICENSES) so future top-level additions cannot silently bypass the residue gate
Self-exclusion symmetric across both literal and templated scans of grep-keystone-api-residue.sh

security_checklist.item: No new external inputs introduced; helper rename is a no-op semantically
Script invokes grep with a fixed pattern set; no caller-influenced interpolation

architecture_checklist.item: Helper name now matches its semantics (returns bare CR name for all sub-resource kinds, not just API)
bootstrapServiceURL still composes the host segment via the helper; no parallel naming convention reintroduced
Residue gate has a structural completeness check (declared_roots vs visible top-level dirs) rather than only an at-runtime existence check

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic introduced; the rename is purely a single-symbol search-and-replace plus three new doc/comment lines
EXCLUDED_ROOTS is the minimum addition required to satisfy the completeness guard — no speculative scaffolding

fail_fast_checklist.item: Script exits 2 (distinct from 1) when a newly added top-level directory is neither scanned nor excluded — surfaces misconfiguration loudly
Script exits 1 with a per-line listing of unmarked residue, not a generic message

defensive_checklist.item: Self-exclusion of the script applied to BOTH literal and templated scans, not just one
Hidden top-level directories (.git, .github, .planwerk) deliberately skipped by the completeness check with a comment explaining why

issues_found.description: Awkward inline 'CC-0095 legacy' marker placed mid-sentence in the subResourceName doc-comment hurts readability. Line 27 reads `// the historical "{name}-api" framing was dropped to align internal Service // CC-0095 legacy: pre-rename name referenced for traceability.` — the second `//` in the middle of a sentence forces the reader to parse a double comment, and the prose continues on the next line as if the marker weren't there. The marker is required only because the line contains the templated literal `{name}-api` that the residue scanner matches. A cleaner alternative is to rephrase the prose to refer to "the historical `-api` suffix" (no templated literal, no marker needed). The current form is functional and the residue scanner accepts it; this is a readability nit, not a correctness defect.
issues_found.fix: Replace the templated literal in the prose with a non-templated description so no inline marker is needed: `// place. Since CC-0095 the helper returns the bare CR name with no suffix — the historical -api suffix was dropped to align internal Service DNS with the public hostname posture established by CC-0088.` Then drop the inline `// CC-0095 legacy: ...` fragment.

suggested_improvements: Consider replacing the templated `{name}-api` literal in the subResourceName doc-comment (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_hpa.go:27) with prose like 'the historical -api suffix' so the residue-scanner marker is not needed mid-sentence; this resolves the only readability nit in this round.

The EXCLUDED_ROOTS array now lists `releases` with the rationale 'generated install manifests; sourced from operators/ which is scanned'. If `releases/` later starts containing hand-edited content rather than purely generated output, that exclusion becomes a silent gap. Consider revisiting if the directory's purpose changes.
next_steps: Optional: apply the AUTO_FIX in reconcile_hpa.go:27 to remove the inline comment placement; everything else is good to merge.
